c1.speaker: dot
c1.line: Gibbons. Before you ask: the ceremony's tonight, and until that vault opens it writes itself. Today's story is smaller. Somebody is stealing my red pens.

press.line: How many pens are we talking about?

observe.line: There's red ink on the stair rail. Small hands.

c3.speaker: dot
c3.line: Twelve. Twelve pens in two weeks — one a shift, and I count my shifts, Gibbons. That's not misplacement, that's a pattern. Find my pattern.

c3e.speaker: dot
c3e.line: I love deadlines, Gibbons. The pens are how I love them. Twelve gone in two weeks, one a shift. Find my pattern.

c3o.speaker: dot
c3o.line: ...Small hands. Hm. You're Gary Gibbons's, all right — he'd have said the same thing before his coffee. Go on then. Follow the ink.

c4.speaker: dot
c4.line: And Gibbons — sell me the story. In one sentence. That's the job. Every morning, that's the job.

c5.speaker: dot
c5.line: Gary Gibbons filed from a rowboat once. One sentence, mid-river, deadline made. …Go earn the rest of that story.

p1.speaker: dot
p1.line: You've got that look. The one where you already followed something. Out with it.
